title: Enterprise AI with Microsoft Semantic Kernel
modules: Semantic Kernel Architecture: Plugins, Planners, and the Kernel Pipeline
Connecting LLMs to Enterprise Systems: Connectors, Memory, and Context Management
Building Reusable AI Plugins for Business Workflows
Orchestrating Multi-Step AI Tasks with Planners and Agents
Security, Governance, and Cost Control in Production Deployments
Integrating Semantic Kernel with Azure OpenAI and Microsoft 365
Testing, Observability, and Debugging AI Pipelines
Applying Semantic Kernel to a Real Enterprise Use Case
synopsis: Semantic Kernel is Microsoft's production-grade SDK for embedding LLM capabilities into enterprise applications — built for .NET, Python, and Java environments already common in large organizations. This course gives software engineers, architects, and technical leads a hands-on path from SDK fundamentals to deploying governed, scalable AI workflows inside real enterprise infrastructure.
rationale: As Microsoft deepens AI integration across its enterprise stack, Semantic Kernel is rapidly becoming the default orchestration layer for corporate AI development — professionals without this skill will cede technical leadership on AI initiatives.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to design, build, and deploy a production-ready AI workflow using Semantic Kernel that integrates with existing enterprise systems and meets organizational governance requirements.
source_signals: Windows 11 adds AI agent that runs in background with access to personal folders (score: 2617)
